docs.title: emacs-r-devkit
docs.text: Professional Emacs environment for R package development on macOS

docs.title: ✨ Features
docs.text:
• ESS Integration

    Full Emacs Speaks Statistics support with R console, code evaluation, and REPL interaction.

  • Flycheck + Linters

    Real-time syntax checking with lintr and custom styler integration for code quality.

  • LSP Mode

    Language Server Protocol support with R languageserver for intelligent code navigation.

  • Smart Completion

    Company mode with context-aware autocompletion for R functions and objects.

  • Roxygen2 Automation

    Automatic documentation skeleton generation with parameter detection.

  • Auto-Formatting

    Styler integration for automatic code formatting on save with error guards.

  • S7 Support

    Built-in snippets and helpers for R's new S7 object-oriented programming system.

  • Usethis Integration

    Quick commands for creating R files, tests, and package documentation.

docs.title: 🚀 Quick Start
docs.text:
InstallationFirst LaunchVerify Setup 
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/Data-Wise/emacs-r-devkit.git
cd emacs-r-devkit

# Run the installer
./install-init.sh

# Check dependencies
./check-dependencies.sh

# Start Emacs (first launch takes 10-15 minutes for package installation)
open -a Emacs

# Or from terminal
emacs

# Open a test R file
emacs test-features.R

# Follow the checklist in TEST-CHECKLIST.md

docs.title: 🎯 Core Capabilities
docs.text: Feature Tool Keybinding Purpose R Console ESS M-x R Start interactive R session Send Code ESS C-RET Execute line/region in R Documentation Roxygen2 C-c r r Insert roxygen skeleton Go to Definition LSP M-. Jump to function definition Find References LSP M-? Find all usages Syntax Check Flycheck C-c ! l List errors/warnings Auto-Format Styler On save Format code automatically Completion Company Automatic Suggest completions

docs.title: 📋 Prerequisites
docs.text:
System Requirements

  • macOS (tested on macOS 12+)
  • Emacs 27+ (emacs-plus recommended)
  • R 4.0+

docs.title: Required R Packages
docs.text:
install.packages(c(
  "devtools",
  "usethis",
  "roxygen2",
  "testthat",
  "lintr",
  "styler"
))

docs.title: Optional R Packages
docs.text:
install.packages(c(
  "languageserver",  # For LSP mode
  "s7",              # For S7 OOP support
  "covr",            # For test coverage
  "pkgdown"          # For package websites
))

docs.title: 🎨 Integrated Workflow
docs.text:
emacs-r-devkit is designed to work seamlessly with your existing R development workflow:

Emacs (Editing)Terminal (Validation)
  • Write and edit R code with syntax highlighting
  • Auto-format with styler on save
  • Generate documentation with roxygen2
  • Navigate code with LSP
  • Test interactively in R console
rtest          # Run package tests
rdoc           # Build documentation
rdev           # Full development cycle
rcheck         # R CMD check

See your r-package-development Claude skill for complete workflow aliases.

docs.title: 📚 Documentation
docs.text:
• Getting Started - Installation and setup guide
  • Features - Detailed feature overview
  • Keybindings - Complete keybinding reference
  • Configuration - Customization options
  • Troubleshooting - Common issues and solutions

docs.title: 🔧 Key Keybindings
docs.text:
Quick Reference

  • Ctrl+G - Cancel/Escape (use when stuck!)
  • Ctrl+X Ctrl+F - Open file
  • Ctrl+X Ctrl+S - Save file
  • Option+X - Execute command (M-x)
  • Ctrl+C R - emacs-r-devkit prefix (shows all options)

macOS Important

Meta key (M-) = Option (⌥), NOT Command! Example: M-x = Option-x, M-. = Option-.

docs.title: 🤝 Integration
docs.text:
Works seamlessly with:

  • zsh-environment - Modern shell with development aliases
  • r-package-development - Complete R workflow automation
  • Claude Code - AI-powered development assistance

docs.title: 🎯 Use Cases
docs.text:
• R package development
  • Statistical computing
  • Data analysis scripts
  • Interactive R exploration
  • Documentation writing
  • Test development

docs.text: MIT License - see LICENSE for details.

docs.title: 🙏 Acknowledgments
docs.text:
Built with:

  • Emacs - The extensible text editor
  • ESS - Emacs Speaks Statistics
  • Material for MkDocs - Documentation theme

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Customize emacs-r-devkit to fit your workflow and preferences.

docs.title: Configuration File
docs.text:
All configuration is in ~/.emacs.d/init.el (616 lines).

Live Reload

After editing init.el, reload with: M-x eval-buffer

docs.title: Globally
docs.text:
;; Add to init.el
(setq emacs-r-devkit/styler-enabled nil)

docs.title: Per Project
docs.text:
Create .dir-locals.el in project root:

((ess-r-mode . ((emacs-r-devkit/styler-enabled . nil))))

docs.title: Per Session
docs.text: Toggle with Ctrl+C R Shift+S

docs.title: Adjust Company Delay
docs.text:
;; Faster completions (default: 0.1)
(setq company-idle-delay 0)

;; Slower completions
(setq company-idle-delay 0.5)

;; Disable automatic completions
(setq company-idle-delay nil)

docs.title: Disable Line Numbers
docs.text:
;; Remove line numbers hook
(remove-hook 'prog-mode-hook #'display-line-numbers-mode)

docs.title: Change Theme
docs.text:
emacs-r-devkit uses modus-vivendi (dark) by default.

;; Light theme
(load-theme 'modus-operandi t)

;; Other popular themes
(use-package doom-themes
  :ensure t
  :config
  (load-theme 'doom-one t))

docs.title: Configure Flycheck
docs.text:
;; Disable lintr checker
(setq-default flycheck-disabled-checkers '(r-lintr))

;; Only run on save (not while typing)
(setq flycheck-check-syntax-automatically '(save mode-enabled))

;; Adjust delay before checking (milliseconds)
(setq flycheck-idle-change-delay 2.0)

docs.title: LSP Performance
docs.text:
;; Increase file watch threshold for large projects
(setq lsp-file-watch-threshold 10000)

;; Disable LSP for specific projects
;; Add to .dir-locals.el:
((ess-r-mode . ((lsp-mode . nil))))

docs.title: Custom Keybindings
docs.text:
;; Add to ESS mode hook
(add-hook 'ess-r-mode-hook
  (lambda ()
    ;; Custom keybinding example
    (define-key ess-r-mode-map (kbd "C-c m") 'my-function)

    ;; Remap existing
    (define-key ess-r-mode-map (kbd "C-c C-r") 'ess-eval-region)))

docs.title: Custom Roxygen Template
docs.text:
;; Modify emacs-r-devkit/insert-roxygen-skeleton function
;; in init.el around line 229-279

;; Example: Add custom fields
(defun emacs-r-devkit/insert-roxygen-skeleton ()
  ;; ... existing code ...
  (insert "#' @author Your Name\n")
  (insert "#' @date " (format-time-string "%Y-%m-%d") "\n")
  ;; ... rest of template ...
)

docs.title: Styler Options
docs.text:
Customize styler behavior by editing ~/.emacs.d/bin/r-styler-check.R:

# Current default:
styled <- styler::style_text(code_lines)

# Custom options:
styled <- styler::style_text(
  code_lines,
  strict = FALSE,           # Less strict
  indent_by = 4,            # 4-space indent
  scope = "line_breaks"     # Only fix line breaks
)

docs.title: Font Configuration
docs.text:
;; Set font and size
(set-frame-font "Monaco 14" nil t)

;; Or with better fallback
(when (find-font (font-spec :name "JetBrains Mono"))
  (set-frame-font "JetBrains Mono 13" nil t))

docs.title: Using .dir-locals.el
docs.text:
Create .dir-locals.el in project root for project-specific settings:

;; Example: Configure for specific package
((ess-r-mode . ((emacs-r-devkit/styler-enabled . nil)
                (flycheck-disabled-checkers . (r-lintr))
                (lsp-mode . nil))))

Common uses:

  • Disable styler for specific package
  • Turn off LSP for large packages
  • Project-specific indentation
  • Custom compile commands

docs.title: Update Packages
docs.text:
M-x package-refresh-contents
M-x package-upgrade-all

docs.title: Install Additional Packages
docs.text:
M-x package-install RET package-name RET

Popular additions:

  • helm - Alternative completion framework
  • ivy - Another completion framework
  • doom-themes - Additional themes
  • company-quickhelp - Inline documentation
  • smartparens - Better paren handling

docs.title: Pin Package Versions
docs.text:
;; Add to init.el
(setq package-pinned-packages
      '((ess . "melpa-stable")))

docs.title: Default R Options
docs.text:
;; Set default R options
(setq inferior-R-args "--no-save --no-restore")

;; Use radian instead of R
(setq inferior-R-program-name "radian")

docs.title: R Startup File
docs.text:
Create ~/.Rprofile for R-specific settings:

# Load commonly used packages
options(
  repos = c(CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org/"),
  browserNLdisabled = TRUE,
  deparse.max.lines = 2
)

if (interactive()) {
  suppressMessages(require(devtools))
  suppressMessages(require(usethis))
}

docs.title: Reduce Startup Time
docs.text:
;; Defer package loading
(use-package package-name
  :defer t  ; Load when needed
  :commands (command1 command2))

;; Increase GC threshold during startup
(setq gc-cons-threshold (* 50 1000 1000))

docs.title: Reduce Memory Usage
docs.text:
;; Lower GC threshold (default is high for performance)
(setq gc-cons-threshold (* 20 1000 1000))

;; Disable features you don't use
(setq lsp-enable-symbol-highlighting nil)
(setq lsp-ui-doc-enable nil)

docs.title: Change Backup Directory
docs.text:
;; Already configured in init.el, but can customize:
(setq backup-directory-alist
      '((".*" . "~/.emacs.d/backups/")))

;; Or disable backups entirely
(setq make-backup-files nil)

docs.title: Auto-Save Configuration
docs.text:
;; Already configured, but can adjust:
(setq auto-save-file-name-transforms
      '((".*" "~/.emacs.d/auto-saves/" t)))

;; Adjust auto-save interval (default: 300 characters)
(setq auto-save-interval 200)

docs.title: Enable Debug Mode
docs.text:
;; Add to top of init.el temporarily
(setq debug-on-error t)

;; See full error traces

docs.title: Profile Startup
docs.text:
M-x emacs-init-time       # Total startup time
M-x profiler-start        # Start profiling
M-x profiler-report       # See results

docs.title: Set Environment Variables
docs.text:
;; Add to init.el
(setenv "R_LIBS_USER" "~/R/library")
(setenv "GITHUB_PAT" "your-token")

;; Or load from file
(when (file-exists-p "~/.emacs.d/env.el")
  (load "~/.emacs.d/env.el"))

docs.title: Configure External Tools
docs.text:
;; Quarto
(setq quarto-program "/opt/homebrew/bin/quarto")

;; Custom R checker
(setq flycheck-r-lintr-linters
      "with_defaults(line_length_linter(120))")

docs.title: Full Reset
docs.text:
# Backup current config
mv ~/.emacs.d ~/.emacs.d.backup

# Reinstall
cd ~/emacs-r-devkit
./install-init.sh

docs.title: Partial Reset
docs.text:
# Just reset packages
rm -rf ~/.emacs.d/elpa/

# Restart Emacs to reinstall

docs.title: Minimal Setup
docs.text:
For low-powered machines:

;; Disable heavy features
(setq emacs-r-devkit/styler-enabled nil)
(setq lsp-mode nil)
(setq company-idle-delay nil)  ; Manual completion only

;; Keep essential features
;; - ESS
;; - Flycheck with lintr only
;; - Basic completion

docs.title: Maximum Features
docs.text:
For powerful machines:

;; Enable all features at max
(setq company-idle-delay 0)
(setq flycheck-idle-change-delay 0.5)
(setq lsp-file-watch-threshold 50000)

;; Add extra packages
(use-package company-quickhelp
  :ensure t
  :config
  (company-quickhelp-mode))

docs.title: Check Current Values
docs.text:
M-: variable-name RET           # Check variable value
C-h v variable-name RET         # Describe variable
C-h f function-name RET         # Describe function

docs.title: Test Configuration
docs.text:
# Test with fresh config
emacs -Q

# Load init.el manually
M-x load-file RET ~/.emacs.d/init.el RET

Next: Check the Troubleshooting guide if you encounter issues.

docs.title: Features
docs.text: emacs-r-devkit provides a complete, integrated development environment for R package development within Emacs.

docs.title: ESS (Emacs Speaks Statistics)
docs.text:
Full integration with R for interactive development.

Capabilities:

  • Interactive R console (M-x R)
  • Send code from buffer to R (C-RET)
  • Function and region evaluation
  • Syntax highlighting for R, Rmd, and Quarto
  • Smart indentation
  • Help system integration

Key Features:

# Send line to R
x <- 1:10              # Cursor here, press C-RET

# Send region to R
summary(mtcars)        # Select region, press C-RET
plot(mpg ~ wt, mtcars)

# Send entire function
my_function <- function(x) {
  result <- x * 2
  return(result)
}  # Cursor in function, press C-c C-c

docs.title: Flycheck Integration
docs.text:
Real-time syntax and style checking with multiple checkers.

Checkers:

  1. lintr - R code linting

    • Syntax errors
    • Style violations
    • Code smells
    • Best practice violations

  2. r-styler - Code style validation

    • Tidyverse style guide compliance
    • Custom styler checker at ~/.emacs.d/bin/r-styler-check.R

In Action:

  • Errors shown with red squiggles
  • Warnings with orange squiggles
  • Info with blue squiggles
  • List all issues: C-c ! l
  • Jump to next: C-c ! n

docs.title: LSP Mode
docs.text:
Language Server Protocol support for intelligent code navigation.

Features:

  • Go to definition (M-.)
  • Find references (M-?)
  • Symbol search
  • Hover documentation
  • Code actions
  • Rename refactoring

Requirements:

  • R package: languageserver
  • Must be in R package (needs DESCRIPTION file)
  • Standalone R files won't activate LSP

Usage:

# Jump to function definition
source_function()     # M-. on this

# Find all usages
my_variable          # M-? on this

# Rename symbol
old_name <- 1        # C-c l r r to rename

docs.title: Company Mode
docs.text:
Context-aware code completion.

Completion Sources:

  • R functions and objects
  • Function arguments
  • File paths
  • R package exports
  • LSP suggestions

Behavior:

  • Automatic popup after short delay (0.1 seconds)
  • Navigate with C-n / C-p
  • Accept with TAB or RET
  • Cancel with C-g
  • Manual trigger: M-x company-complete

docs.title: Roxygen2 Integration
docs.text:
Automatic documentation skeleton generation with parameter detection.

Command: C-c r r

Example:

# Before - your function
calculate_stats <- function(data,
                           method = "mean",
                           na.rm = TRUE) {
  # implementation
}

# After pressing C-c r r (cursor before function)
#' Title
#'
#' @param data
#' @param method
#' @param na.rm
#'
#' @return
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
calculate_stats <- function(data,
                           method = "mean",
                           na.rm = TRUE) {
  # implementation
}

Features:

  • Detects function parameters (handles complex signatures)
  • Supports multiline parameter lists
  • Extracts parameter names (ignores defaults)
  • Can use selected region as @examples

docs.title: Styler Auto-Format
docs.text:
Automatic code formatting on save using the styler R package.

Behavior:

  • Runs on every save (C-x C-s)
  • Formats entire buffer
  • Preserves cursor position
  • Guards against syntax errors
  • Toggle with C-c r S

What It Fixes:

  • Indentation
  • Spacing around operators
  • Line breaks
  • Trailing whitespace
  • Brace placement

Example:

# Before save (messy)
my_func=function(x,y){
result=x+y
return(result)
}

# After save (clean)
my_func <- function(x, y) {
  result <- x + y
  return(result)
}

Disable for Project:

Create .dir-locals.el in project root:

((ess-r-mode . ((emacs-r-devkit/styler-enabled . nil))))

docs.title: Usethis Integration
docs.text:
Quick commands for R package scaffolding.

Commands:

  • C-c r u - Create R file (usethis::use_r())
  • C-c r t - Create test file (usethis::use_test())
  • C-c r p - Create package doc (usethis::use_package_doc())

Example:

C-c r u
Prompt: utils
Creates: R/utils.R

C-c r t
Prompt: utils
Creates: tests/testthat/test-utils.R

docs.title: S7 Support
docs.text:
Built-in snippets and helpers for R's new S7 OOP system.

Commands:

  • C-c r s c - Insert S7 class definition
  • C-c r s m - Insert S7 method
  • C-c r s g - Insert S7 generic

Example Class:

# After C-c r s c
MyClass <- S7::new_class(
  name = "MyClass",
  properties = list(
    field1 = class_integer,
    field2 = class_character
  ),
  validator = function(self) {
    # Validation logic
  }
)

docs.title: Magit (Git Integration)
docs.text:
Professional Git interface within Emacs.

Launch: C-x g

Common Operations:

  • s - Stage file
  • u - Unstage file
  • c c - Commit
  • P p - Push
  • F p - Pull
  • q - Quit magit

docs.title: Projectile (Project Management)
docs.text:
Navigate and manage projects efficiently.

Key Commands:

  • C-c p f - Find file in project
  • C-c p p - Switch project
  • C-c p s s - Search in project

docs.title: Which-Key
docs.text:
Visual keybinding guide.

Usage:

  • Press C-c r and wait
  • Popup shows all available commands
  • Shows keybindings and descriptions
  • Helps discover functionality

docs.title: Vertico/Consult/Marginalia
docs.text:
Modern completion framework for enhanced UX.

Features:

  • Vertical completion display (Vertico)
  • Enhanced search commands (Consult)
  • Rich annotations (Marginalia)
  • Fuzzy matching
  • Preview support

docs.title: PATH Management
docs.text:
Automatic PATH synchronization for GUI Emacs.

How It Works:

  • Uses exec-path-from-shell package
  • Loads PATH from shell at startup
  • Ensures R, Quarto, etc. are found

Manual Export:

C-c r P         # Export PATH

Or run:

~/.emacs.d/bin/export-gui-path.sh

docs.title: Keybinding Optimization
docs.text:
macOS-friendly keybindings:

  • Option (⌥) = Meta (M-)
  • Command (⌘) = Super (S-)
  • Right-Option available for special chars

docs.title: On-Demand Loading
docs.text:
Packages load only when needed (use-package with :defer):

  • Fast startup (<5 seconds after initial install)
  • Minimal memory footprint
  • Lazy loading of heavy packages

docs.title: Optimized Defaults
docs.text:
Pre-configured for performance:

  • Disabled automatic backups in working directory
  • Custom auto-save directory (~/.emacs.d/auto-saves/)
  • Disabled toolbar, scrollbar for cleaner UI
  • Line numbers only for programming modes

docs.title: Smart Caching
docs.text:
• Recent files tracking (recentf)
  • File name cache
  • LSP workspace caching

docs.title: Workflow Integration
docs.text: emacs-r-devkit integrates seamlessly with:

docs.title: Terminal Workflow
docs.text:
# Terminal (validation)
rtest          # Run tests
rdoc           # Build docs
rcheck         # R CMD check

# Emacs (editing)
C-x C-f        # Open file
C-c r r        # Add roxygen
C-x C-s        # Save (auto-format)

docs.title: Claude Code Integration
docs.text:
Works with Claude Code for AI assistance:

  • Access via ~/.claude/skills/emacs-r-devkit.md
  • Context-aware help
  • Workflow automation

docs.title: Quarto Support
docs.text:
Full Quarto integration:

  • Syntax highlighting for .qmd files
  • Code execution
  • Preview support

docs.title: Customization Points
docs.text:
All major features can be customized:

  • Disable auto-formatting per-project
  • Adjust company delay
  • Configure LSP settings
  • Customize keybindings
  • Add custom snippets

See Configuration for details.

docs.title: Feature Comparison
docs.text:
Feature Without emacs-r-devkit With emacs-r-devkit Code Editing Basic text editing Syntax highlighting, smart indentation Documentation Manual roxygen Auto-generated skeletons Style Checking Manual styler::style_file() Automatic on save Navigation Manual search Jump to definition, find refs Completion None Context-aware suggestions Error Detection After R CMD check Real-time in editor Git Command-line only Visual interface (Magit) R Console Separate terminal Integrated in Emacs

Next: Learn the Keybindings to use these features effectively.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide will walk you through installing and setting up emacs-r-devkit on your macOS system.

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text:
• macOS 12.0 or later
  • Emacs 27.1 or later (emacs-plus recommended)
  • R 4.0 or later
  • Homebrew (recommended for package management)

docs.title: Install Emacs
docs.text:
emacs-plus (Recommended)Vanilla Emacs 
# Install emacs-plus with native compilation
brew tap d12frosted/emacs-plus
brew install emacs-plus@30 --with-native-comp

# Link to Applications
ln -s /opt/homebrew/opt/emacs-plus@30/Emacs.app /Applications/Emacs.app

# Install standard Emacs
brew install emacs

# Or download from https://emacsformacosx.com/

docs.title: Install R
docs.text:
# Install R via Homebrew
brew install r

# Verify installation
R --version

docs.title: Step 1: Clone Repository
docs.text:
cd ~/
git clone https://github.com/Data-Wise/emacs-r-devkit.git
cd emacs-r-devkit

docs.title: Step 2: Backup Existing Configuration
docs.text:
Important

If you have an existing Emacs configuration, back it up first!

# Backup existing config
mv ~/.emacs.d ~/.emacs.d.backup
mv ~/.emacs ~/.emacs.backup 2>/dev/null || true

docs.title: Step 3: Run Installer
docs.text:
# Make installer executable
chmod +x install-init.sh

# Run installer
./install-init.sh

The installer will:

  • Copy init.el to ~/.emacs.d/init.el
  • Copy helper scripts to ~/.emacs.d/bin/
  • Set proper permissions

docs.title: Step 4: Install R Packages
docs.text:
# Required packages
install.packages(c(
  "devtools",
  "usethis",
  "roxygen2",
  "testthat",
  "lintr",
  "styler"
))

# Optional but recommended
install.packages(c(
  "languageserver",  # LSP support
  "s7",              # S7 OOP system
  "covr",            # Test coverage
  "pkgdown",         # Package websites
  "remotes"          # Remote package installation
))

docs.title: Step 5: Verify Dependencies
docs.text:
# Run dependency checker
./check-dependencies.sh

Expected output:

================================================
  emacs-r-devkit Dependency Checker
================================================

System Requirements:
-------------------
✓ Emacs: 30.0.92
✓ R: 4.4.2
✓ Rscript: 4.4.2

Required R Packages:
--------------------
✓ R package devtools: 2.4.5
✓ R package usethis: 3.0.0
✓ R package roxygen2: 7.3.2
✓ R package testthat: 3.2.1
✓ R package lintr: 3.1.2
✓ R package styler: 1.10.3

================================================
  Summary
================================================
✓ All dependencies satisfied!

docs.title: Start Emacs
docs.text:
# From terminal
emacs

# Or GUI
open -a Emacs

First Launch Patience

The first launch takes 10-15 minutes as Emacs downloads and compiles packages from MELPA. This is normal!

You'll see: Contacting host: melpa.org

docs.title: Monitor Progress
docs.text:
While Emacs loads, you can monitor progress:

  1. Press Ctrl+H E to see the *Messages* buffer
  2. Look for package installation messages
  3. Wait for Loading ~/.emacs.d/init.el...done

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
Once loaded, check that all modes are active:

  1. Open a test R file: Ctrl+X Ctrl+F test-features.R
  2. Look at the mode line (bottom of window):
    • Should show: (ESS[R] Flycheck LSP company) or similar
  3. Try the Which-Key menu: Ctrl+C R
    • Should show a popup with available R commands

docs.title: Interactive Test
docs.text:
# Open the test file
emacs test-features.R

Follow the instructions in test-features.R to test:

  • Syntax highlighting
  • Code completion
  • Flycheck integration
  • Roxygen insertion
  • LSP navigation
  • R console integration

docs.title: Full Checklist
docs.text:
For a comprehensive test, see:

  • TEST-CHECKLIST.md - Complete verification checklist
  • test-roxygen.R - Test roxygen skeleton generation

docs.title: Start R Console
docs.text:
1. Press Option+X (that's M-x)
  2. Type R and press Enter
  3. Wait for R to start

You should see:

R version 4.4.2 (2024-10-31) -- "Pile of Leaves"
...
>

docs.title: Send Code to R
docs.text:
1. Open an R file: Ctrl+X Ctrl+F test.R
  2. Type some R code: x <- 1:10
  3. With cursor on that line, press Ctrl+Enter
  4. Code should execute in R console

docs.title: macOS PATH Configuration
docs.text: GUI Emacs on macOS often can't find command-line tools. emacs-r-devkit handles this automatically, but you can verify:

docs.title: Check PATH
docs.text:
In Emacs, press Option+Shift+; (that's M-:) and type:

(getenv "PATH")

Should include /usr/local/bin or /opt/homebrew/bin.

docs.title: Manual PATH Export
docs.text:
If R is not found:

# In Emacs
C-c r P         # Export PATH to GUI

# Or in terminal
~/.emacs.d/bin/export-gui-path.sh

Then restart Emacs.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Now that you're set up:

  1. Learn Keybindings - See Keybindings Reference
  2. Explore Features - See Features
  3. Customize - See Configuration
  4. Start Coding - Create your first R package!

docs.title: Common First-Time Issues
docs.text:
Emacs won't start

Check ~/.emacs.d/init.el exists. If not, re-run ./install-init.sh.

R not found

Run C-c r P in Emacs to export PATH. See Troubleshooting.

Package errors

Delete ~/.emacs.d/elpa/ and restart Emacs to reinstall packages.

Slow startup

First launch is slow (10-15 min). Subsequent launches are fast (<5 seconds).

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
• Troubleshooting Guide - Solutions to common problems
  • GitHub Issues - Report bugs
  • Press Ctrl+H K then any key to see what it does

docs.title: Tutorial Resources
docs.text:
For complete tutorials, see:

  • TUTORIAL.md in the repository
  • CHEAT-SHEET.md for quick reference
  • Your emacs-r-devkit Claude skill

Ready to code! Open an R file and start developing with the power of Emacs.

docs.title: Keybindings Reference
docs.text: Complete keybinding guide for emacs-r-devkit on macOS.

docs.title: Keybindings Reference
docs.text:
Complete keybinding guide for emacs-r-devkit on macOS.

macOS Important

Meta key (M-) = Option (⌥), NOT Command! Example: M-x means press Option-x, M-. means press Option-.

docs.title: Key Notation
docs.text: Notation macOS Key Description C- Control Control key M- Option (⌥) Meta/Option key ← Important! S- Command (⌘) Super/Command key Shift Shift Shift key RET Return Return/Enter SPC Space Spacebar TAB Tab Tab key

docs.title: Survival Keys (Learn First!)
docs.text:
Emergency Exit

If you get stuck, press Ctrl+G multiple times to cancel everything!

Key Action Description Ctrl+G Cancel Escape/cancel current command Ctrl+X Ctrl+F Find file Open a file Ctrl+X Ctrl+S Save Save current file Ctrl+X Ctrl+C Exit Quit Emacs Option+X Execute Run a command by name

docs.title: Basic Movement
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+A Beginning of line Ctrl+E End of line Ctrl+N Next line Ctrl+P Previous line Ctrl+F Forward character Ctrl+B Backward character

docs.title: Word/Sentence Movement
docs.text: Key Action Option+F Forward word Option+B Backward word Option+Shift+< Beginning of buffer Option+Shift+> End of buffer

docs.title: Page Movement
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+V Page down Option+V Page up Option+G G Go to line number

docs.title: Cut, Copy, Paste
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+Space Start selection (mark) Ctrl+W Cut (kill region) Option+W Copy Ctrl+Y Paste (yank) Ctrl+K Cut to end of line Option+D Delete word forward

docs.title: Undo
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+/ Undo Ctrl+X U Undo (alternative)

docs.title: File Operations
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+X Ctrl+F Find/open file Ctrl+X Ctrl+S Save file Ctrl+X Ctrl+W Save as

docs.title: Buffer Operations
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+X B Switch buffer Ctrl+X K Kill (close) buffer Ctrl+X Ctrl+B List all buffers

docs.title: Window Management
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+X 2 Split horizontally Ctrl+X 3 Split vertically Ctrl+X 1 Close other windows Ctrl+X 0 Close current window Ctrl+X O Switch to other window

docs.title: Search & Replace
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+S Search forward (isearch) Ctrl+R Search backward Ctrl+S Ctrl+S Search again Option+Shift+5 Query replace

docs.title: Help System
docs.text: Key Action Description Ctrl+H K Describe key What does this key do? Ctrl+H F Describe function Function documentation Ctrl+H V Describe variable Variable documentation Ctrl+H M Describe mode Current mode info Ctrl+H E View messages Show *Messages* buffer

docs.title: emacs-r-devkit Prefix (C-c r)
docs.text:
Discovery

Press Ctrl+C R and wait 1 second - Which-Key will show all available commands!

docs.title: Core Commands
docs.text: Key Action Description Ctrl+C R R Roxygen Insert roxygen skeleton Ctrl+C R Shift+S Toggle styler Enable/disable auto-format Ctrl+C R Shift+P Export PATH Fix macOS PATH issues

docs.title: Usethis Integration
docs.text: Key Action Description Ctrl+C R U Use R Create R file (use_r()) Ctrl+C R T Use test Create test file (use_test()) Ctrl+C R P Package doc Create package doc

docs.title: S7 Integration
docs.text: Key Action Description Ctrl+C R S C S7 class Insert class definition Ctrl+C R S M S7 method Insert method Ctrl+C R S G S7 generic Insert generic

docs.title: R Console
docs.text: Key Action Option+X R Start R console Ctrl+Enter Send line/region to R Ctrl+C Ctrl+C Send function to R Ctrl+C Ctrl+B Send buffer to R Ctrl+C Ctrl+Z Switch to R console Ctrl+C Ctrl+L Load file in R

docs.title: R Help
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+C Ctrl+V R help on object

docs.title: Flycheck (Syntax Checking)
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+C ! L List all errors Ctrl+C ! N Next error Ctrl+C ! P Previous error Ctrl+C ! ? Describe checker Ctrl+C ! V Verify setup

docs.title: LSP Mode
docs.text: Key Action Option+. Go to definition Option+, Go back Option+? Find references Ctrl+C L R R Rename symbol Ctrl+C L G G Find definition

docs.title: Company (Completion)
docs.text: Key Action (automatic) Completions appear Ctrl+N Next completion Ctrl+P Previous completion Tab or Enter Accept completion Ctrl+G Cancel completion Option+X company-complete Manual trigger

docs.title: Launch
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+X G Magit status

docs.title: In Magit Buffer
docs.text: Key Action S Stage file U Unstage file C C Commit Shift+P P Push Shift+F P Pull Q Quit magit Tab Toggle diff

docs.title: Projectile
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+C P F Find file in project Ctrl+C P P Switch project Ctrl+C P S S Search in project

docs.title: Quarto
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+C Ctrl+C Execute chunk

docs.title: Common Workflows
docs.text:
Add DocumentationNavigate CodeFix ErrorsTest in R 
1. C-c r r          # Insert roxygen
2. Fill in fields
3. C-x C-s          # Save (auto-formats)

1. M-.              # Jump to definition
2. Explore code
3. M-,              # Jump back

1. C-c ! l          # List errors
2. C-c ! n          # Next error
3. Fix code
4. C-x C-s          # Save

1. C-RET            # Send line to R
2. Check output in R console
3. C-c C-z          # Jump to R console

docs.title: Common Mistakes
docs.text:
Using Command instead of Option

M-x is Option-x, NOT Command-x M-. is Option-., NOT Command-.

Forgetting to hold Control

C-x C-f requires holding Control for both: Ctrl-x then Ctrl-f

Not using C-g

When stuck, press C-g repeatedly! It cancels everything.

Trying to use mouse

Emacs is keyboard-first. Learn the keybindings!

docs.title: Customizing Keybindings
docs.text:
To add custom keybindings, edit ~/.emacs.d/init.el:

;; Example: Add custom keybinding
(define-key ess-r-mode-map (kbd "C-c m") 'my-custom-function)

;; Example: Remap existing key
(define-key ess-r-mode-map (kbd "C-c C-r") 'ess-eval-region)

docs.title: Keybinding Conflicts
docs.text:
If you have conflicts with macOS system shortcuts:

System Preferences → Keyboard → Shortcuts

Consider disabling:

  • Mission Control shortcuts
  • Spotlight shortcuts
  • Input Source shortcuts

These often conflict with Emacs M- (Option) keys.

docs.title: Quick Reference Card
docs.text:
Print this table for your desk:

Task Keys Open file C-x C-f Save C-x C-s Cancel C-g Undo C-/ Start R M-x R Send to R C-RET Roxygen C-c r r Go to def M-. List errors C-c ! l Help C-h k

docs.title: Learning Strategy
docs.text:
1. Week 1: Master survival keys (C-g, C-x C-f, C-x C-s)
  2. Week 2: Learn movement (C-a, C-e, M-f, M-b)
  3. Week 3: Learn editing (C-w, M-w, C-y)
  4. Week 4: Learn R workflow (C-RET, C-c r r, M-.)
  5. Week 5+: Explore advanced features

Next: Learn how to configure emacs-r-devkit for your needs.

docs.title: Obsidian Research Lab Integration
docs.text: The emacs-r-devkit is designed to work seamlessly with the Obsidian CLI Ops (obs) tool, creating a powerful "Lab Notebook" workflow. This integration allows you to push results from your R environment directly into your permanent Knowledge Base.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
1. Obsidian CLI Ops: You must have the obs CLI installed.
    • Installation Guide
  2. Federated Vault: Your Obsidian vault should be structured with a Research_Lab folder (standard in the Data-Wise ecosystem).

docs.title: The Workflow
docs.text:
The core philosophy is simple: Emacs is for Computation, Obsidian is for Memory.

Activity Tool Writing Code Emacs (ESS) Running Simulations R Archiving Plots obs log Drafting Vignettes obs draft Checking Math obs context

docs.title: Emacs Configuration
docs.text:
Add the following Elisp functions to your init.el or config.el to expose the obs CLI powers directly inside Emacs.

(defgroup obsidian-ops nil
  "Settings for Obsidian CLI Ops integration."
  :group 'tools)

(defun obs-r-link-project (obsidian-path)
  "Link the current R project to a specific Obsidian folder."
  (interactive "sObsidian Folder Path (e.g., Research_Lab/MyProject): ")
  (shell-command (format "obs r-dev link \"%s\"" obsidian-path))
  (message "Linked current project to %s" obsidian-path))

(defun obs-log-file (file description)
  "Log a specific file to the Obsidian Lab Notebook (06_Analysis)."
  (interactive "fFile to log: \nsDescription: ")
  (let ((cmd (format "obs r-dev log \"%s\" -m \"%s\""
                     (expand-file-name file)
                     description)))
    (shell-command cmd)
    (message "Logged %s to Obsidian." (file-name-nondirectory file))))

(defun obs-fetch-theory (term)
  "Search the Knowledge Base for a theoretical concept and display it."
  (interactive "sSearch Concept: ")
  (let ((result (shell-command-to-string (format "obs r-dev context \"%s\"" term))))
    (with-current-buffer (get-buffer-create "*Obsidian Context*")
      (erase-buffer)
      (insert result)
      (markdown-mode)
      (display-buffer (current-buffer)))))

;; Keybindings
(global-set-key (kbd "C-c o l") 'obs-r-link-project)
(global-set-key (kbd "C-c o f") 'obs-log-file)
(global-set-key (kbd "C-c o t") 'obs-fetch-theory)

docs.title: 1. Saving a Plot
docs.text:
After generating a plot in R:

  1. Save it: ggsave("results/simulation_v1.png")
  2. In Emacs: M-x obs-log-file -> Select results/simulation_v1.png -> Desc: "Run with n=500"
  3. Result: The file is copied to your Obsidian project's 06_Analysis folder, timestamped and safe.

docs.title: 2. Checking Formulas
docs.text:
You are writing a function for the Sobel Test and forget the standard error formula.

  1. In Emacs: C-c o t (or M-x obs-fetch-theory)
  2. Type: "Sobel Standard Error"
  3. Result: A buffer opens showing the relevant Markdown snippet from your Knowledge_Base, complete with LaTeX formulas.

docs.title: 3. Drafting Documentation
docs.text:
You want to write the prose for your package vignette in Obsidian's distraction-free editor.

  1. Create draft in R: usethis::use_vignette("intro")
  2. Run in terminal (or shell-command): obs r-dev draft vignettes/intro.Rmd
  3. Result: The file appears in Research_Lab/.../02_Drafts. You write there. When done, you copy it back (or use rsync).

docs.title: Testing Guide
docs.text: Verify your emacs-r-devkit installation is working correctly.

docs.title: Automated Check
docs.text:
# Run dependency checker
./check-dependencies.sh

Expected output:

================================================
  emacs-r-devkit Dependency Checker
================================================

System Requirements:
-------------------
✓ Emacs: 30.0.92
✓ R: 4.4.2
✓ Rscript: 4.4.2

Required R Packages:
--------------------
✓ R package devtools: 2.4.5
✓ R package usethis: 3.0.0
...

================================================
  Summary
================================================
✓ All dependencies satisfied!

docs.title: Test File Method
docs.text:
Use the provided test file:

emacs test-features.R

Follow instructions in the file to test:

  1. Syntax highlighting
  2. Code completion
  3. Flycheck integration
  4. Roxygen insertion
  5. LSP navigation
  6. R console integration
  7. Auto-formatting

docs.title: 1. Basic Emacs Functions
docs.text:
File OperationsNavigation 
C-x C-f test.R RET       # Open/create file
# Type some text
C-x C-s                  # Save
C-x k RET                # Close buffer

✅ Expected: File created and saved successfully

# Open any R file
C-a                      # Go to beginning of line
C-e                      # Go to end of line
M-f                      # Forward word (Option-f)
M-b                      # Backward word (Option-b)

✅ Expected: Cursor moves as expected

docs.title: 2. ESS (R Mode)
docs.text:
R ConsoleCode ExecutionFunction Execution 
M-x R RET                # Start R

✅ Expected: 

R version 4.4.2 (2024-10-31) -- "Pile of Leaves"
...
>


# In R file, type:
x <- 1:10

# With cursor on line:
C-RET                    # Send to R

✅ Expected: Code executes in R console, x is defined

# Type a function:
my_func <- function(x) {
  x * 2
}

# Cursor anywhere in function:
C-c C-c                  # Send function to R

✅ Expected: Function defined in R

docs.title: 3. Flycheck
docs.text:
Syntax Error DetectionStyle Warnings 
# Type invalid R code:
x <- 1:10
y <- x +                 # Incomplete

✅ Expected: - Red squiggle under error - FlyC in mode line shows errors - C-c ! l lists errors

# Type poorly formatted code:
x=1:10                   # Should use <-
if(x>5){print(x)}       # Poor spacing

✅ Expected: - Orange squiggles for style issues - lintr warnings shown

docs.title: 4. Company Completion
docs.text:
Function CompletionArgument Completion 
# Start typing:
mea

✅ Expected: - Popup appears with mean, median, etc. - Navigate with C-n/C-p - Accept with TAB or RET

# Type:
mean(

✅ Expected: - Popup shows function arguments - x, trim, na.rm options

docs.title: 5. LSP Mode
docs.text:
Requirements

LSP only works in R packages (needs DESCRIPTION file)

Create Test PackageGo to DefinitionFind References 
# In R console:
usethis::create_package("~/test-pkg")

# In Emacs:
C-x C-f ~/test-pkg/R/utils.R RET

# In R/utils.R:
helper <- function(x) {
  mean(x)
}

main <- function(data) {
  helper(data)           # M-. here
}

✅ Expected: - M-. on helper jumps to definition - M-, returns to original location

# With cursor on `helper`:
M-?                      # Find references

✅ Expected: Shows all usages of helper

docs.title: 6. Roxygen Integration
docs.text:
Basic FunctionComplex Function 
# Type (or open test-roxygen.R):
calculate_mean <- function(x, na.rm = TRUE) {
  mean(x, na.rm = na.rm)
}

# Move cursor to line before function
C-c r r                  # Insert roxygen

✅ Expected: 

#' Title
#'
#' @param x
#' @param na.rm
#'
#' @return
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
calculate_mean <- function(x, na.rm = TRUE) {
  mean(x, na.rm = na.rm)
}


# Multiline parameters:
complex_func <- function(data,
                         method = "mean",
                         na.rm = TRUE,
                         verbose = FALSE) {
  # implementation
}

C-c r r

✅ Expected: All parameters detected (data, method, na.rm, verbose)

docs.title: 7. Styler Auto-Format
8. Usethis Integration
docs.text:
Requirements

Must be in R package directory

Create R FileCreate Test File 
# In R package:
C-c r u
Name: utils RET

✅ Expected: Creates R/utils.R

C-c r t
Name: utils RET

✅ Expected: Creates tests/testthat/test-utils.R

docs.title: 9. S7 Integration
docs.text:
Insert ClassInsert Method 
C-c r s c

✅ Expected: S7 class template inserted

C-c r s m

✅ Expected: S7 method template inserted

docs.title: 10. Which-Key
docs.text:
Prefix Discovery 
C-c r                    # Wait 1 second

✅ Expected: Popup shows all C-c r options

docs.title: Systematic Checklist
docs.text:
Use TEST-CHECKLIST.md for comprehensive verification:

emacs TEST-CHECKLIST.md

Check off each item as you verify it works.

docs.title: In R Package
docs.text:
Create a test package to verify all features:

# In R console
usethis::create_package("~/emacs-test-pkg")

Then test:

  1. ✅ LSP mode activates
  2. ✅ Go to definition works
  3. ✅ C-c r u creates R files
  4. ✅ C-c r t creates test files
  5. ✅ Roxygen insertion works
  6. ✅ Auto-formatting works
  7. ✅ Flycheck shows errors

docs.title: Standalone R File
docs.text:
Test features that work without package:

emacs ~/test-standalone.R
  1. ✅ Syntax highlighting
  2. ✅ Code completion (basic)
  3. ✅ R console integration
  4. ✅ Flycheck linting
  5. ✅ Auto-formatting
  6. ✅ Roxygen insertion

❌ LSP won't work (needs package)

docs.title: Startup Time
docs.text:
# Measure startup
time emacs --eval '(kill-emacs)'

✅ Expected: < 5 seconds (after initial package installation)

docs.title: Large File Handling
docs.text:
# Create large R file
writeLines(rep("x <- 1:10", 1000), "large.R")

# Open in Emacs
emacs large.R

✅ Expected: - Loads quickly - Syntax highlighting works - No lag when typing

docs.title: If ESS Test Fails
docs.text: M-x package-install RET ess RET

docs.title: If Flycheck Test Fails
docs.text:
Rscript -e 'install.packages(c("lintr", "styler"))'
M-x flycheck-verify-setup

docs.title: If LSP Test Fails
docs.text:
install.packages("languageserver")

Ensure you're in R package (has DESCRIPTION).

docs.title: If Roxygen Test Fails
docs.text:
Check function exists:

M-: (fboundp 'emacs-r-devkit/insert-roxygen-skeleton) RET

Should return t.

docs.title: If Styler Test Fails
docs.text:
Rscript -e 'install.packages("styler")'
Rscript ~/.emacs.d/bin/r-styler-check.R test.R

docs.title: Reporting Issues
docs.text:
If tests fail:

  1. Run ./check-dependencies.sh
  2. Check C-h e (Messages buffer)
  3. Note exact error messages
  4. Report to GitHub Issues with:
    • Emacs version (M-x emacs-version)
    • R version (R --version)
    • macOS version
    • Failed test description
    • Error messages

docs.title: Success Criteria
docs.text:
All tests passing means:

✅ emacs-r-devkit is correctly installed ✅ All dependencies satisfied ✅ R integration working ✅ Code navigation functional ✅ Auto-formatting operational ✅ Documentation generation ready ✅ Ready for R package development!

Next: Start developing with the Keybindings Reference!

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Solutions to common issues with emacs-r-devkit on macOS.

Quick Diagnosis

Run the dependency checker first: 

./check-dependencies.sh
This identifies most common problems automatically.

docs.title: init.el Not Found
docs.text:
Symptom: Emacs starts but emacs-r-devkit features don't work

Solution:

# Check if init.el exists
ls -la ~/.emacs.d/init.el

# If missing, run installer
cd ~/emacs-r-devkit
./install-init.sh

# Verify installation
ls -la ~/.emacs.d/init.el
ls -la ~/.emacs.d/bin/

docs.title: Conflicting Configuration
docs.text:
Symptom: Emacs won't start, shows errors about packages

Solution:

# Backup existing config
mv ~/.emacs.d ~/.emacs.d.backup
mv ~/.emacs ~/.emacs.backup 2>/dev/null || true

# Clean install
./install-init.sh

# Restart Emacs
open -a Emacs

docs.title: Slow First Launch
docs.text:
Symptom: First launch takes > 15 minutes

Expected: This is normal! Package installation takes 10-15 minutes.

Solutions:

  1. Be patient - watch for Contacting host: melpa.org
  2. Check progress: Ctrl+H E (Messages buffer)
  3. If truly stuck (>30 min):
# Kill Emacs
killall Emacs emacs

# Delete package cache
rm -rf ~/.emacs.d/elpa/

# Restart (will re-download)
open -a Emacs

docs.title: Package Installation Errors
docs.text:
Symptom: 

Error: Package ... is unavailable

Solution:

In Emacs:
M-x package-refresh-contents RET
M-x package-install RET use-package RET

Or clean install:

rm -rf ~/.emacs.d/elpa/
open -a Emacs

docs.title: No Syntax Highlighting
docs.text:
Symptom: R files show as plain text, no colors

Diagnosis:

In Emacs with .R file open:
M-: (package-installed-p 'ess) RET

Solutions:

If ESS not installed:

M-x package-install RET ess RET
M-x ess-r-mode RET

If ESS installed but not activating:

M-x ess-r-mode RET

docs.title: Can't Start R Console
docs.text:
Symptom:

M-x R
Error: No such file or directory: R

Diagnosis:

# In terminal
which R
echo $PATH

# In Emacs
M-: (getenv "PATH") RET
M-: (executable-find "R") RET

Solutions:

macOS GUI PATH issue:

In Emacs:
C-c r P                # Export PATH

# Or in terminal:
~/.emacs.d/bin/export-gui-path.sh

# Then restart Emacs

Verify PATH includes /usr/local/bin or /opt/homebrew/bin.

docs.title: Flycheck Not Working
docs.text:
Symptom: No error highlighting, no FlyC in mode line

Diagnosis:

In Emacs:
M-x flycheck-verify-setup RET

Solutions:

Enable Flycheck:

M-x flycheck-mode RET
M-x global-flycheck-mode RET

Install lintr:

Rscript -e 'install.packages("lintr")'

Install styler:

Rscript -e 'install.packages("styler")'

docs.title: Styler Checker Fails
docs.text:
Symptom:

STYLER-ERROR: styler package not installed

Solution:

# Install styler
Rscript -e 'install.packages("styler")'

# Test manually
Rscript ~/.emacs.d/bin/r-styler-check.R test.R
echo $?  # Should be 0 or 1, not 2

docs.title: LSP Not Starting
docs.text:
Symptom: No LSP in mode line, M-. doesn't work

Diagnosis:

# Check languageserver installed
Rscript -e 'library(languageserver)'

Solutions:

Install languageserver:

install.packages("languageserver")

Important: LSP only works in R packages!

  • Must have DESCRIPTION file
  • Standalone .R files won't activate LSP

Force LSP start (in R package):

M-x lsp RET

Check LSP session:

M-x lsp-describe-session RET

docs.title: LSP Is Slow
docs.text:
Symptom: Completions take forever, Emacs freezes

Solutions:

# Disconnect LSP temporarily
M-x lsp-disconnect RET

# Or disable for large files (add to init.el):
(setq lsp-file-watch-threshold 10000)

docs.title: Save Fails with Styler Error
docs.text:
Symptom:

Styler failed: Error in parse(...)

Cause: Syntax errors in R code

Solutions:

  1. Disable styler temporarily:
C-c r S                # Toggle off
  1. Fix syntax errors
  2. Re-enable:
C-c r S                # Toggle on

docs.title: Don't Want Auto-Formatting
docs.text:
Solutions:

Disable globally (add to init.el):

(setq emacs-r-devkit/styler-enabled nil)

Disable for specific project (.dir-locals.el):

((ess-r-mode . ((emacs-r-devkit/styler-enabled . nil))))

Toggle per session:

C-c r S

docs.title: No Completions
docs.text:
Symptom: Type code, nothing happens

Diagnosis:

M-: company-mode RET

Solutions:

M-x global-company-mode RET

Adjust delay (add to init.el):

(setq company-idle-delay 0)  # Immediate

docs.title: M- Keys Don't Work
docs.text:
Symptom: M-f doesn't move forward, M-. doesn't work

Diagnosis:

C-h k M-f

Should say M-f runs ...

Solutions:

On macOS: M- = Option (⌥), NOT Command!

Try Option-f instead of Command-f.

Verify configuration:

M-: mac-option-modifier RET

Should show: meta

If wrong, restart Emacs.

docs.title: C-c r Doesn't Work
docs.text:
Symptom: C-c r r does nothing

Diagnosis:

C-h k C-c r

Solutions:

Check which-key:

M-x which-key-mode RET

Verify functions loaded:

M-: (fboundp 'emacs-r-devkit/insert-roxygen-skeleton) RET

Should return t. If nil:

M-x eval-buffer RET  # In init.el

docs.title: GUI Emacs Can't Find R
docs.text:
Symptom: M-x R → "R not found" (but terminal finds R fine)

Diagnosis:

# Terminal
which R
echo $PATH

# Emacs
M-: (getenv "PATH") RET
M-: (executable-find "R") RET

Solutions:

Export PATH:

In Emacs: C-c r P

Or terminal:
~/.emacs.d/bin/export-gui-path.sh

Verify exec-path-from-shell:

M-: (package-installed-p 'exec-path-from-shell) RET

Manual PATH fix (add to init.el):

(setenv "PATH" (concat (getenv "PATH") ":/usr/local/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin"))
(setq exec-path (append exec-path '("/usr/local/bin" "/opt/homebrew/bin")))

docs.title: Emacs Is Slow
docs.text:
Symptoms: Typing lags, Flycheck slows down, LSP freezes

Solutions:

Disable Flycheck temporarily:

M-x flycheck-mode RET

Disable LSP:

M-x lsp-disconnect RET

Reduce company delay:

(setq company-idle-delay 0.2)

Check what's slow:

M-x profiler-start RET
# Work for a bit
M-x profiler-report RET

docs.title: Check Messages
docs.text:
C-h e                    # *Messages* buffer
C-h w                    # *Warnings* (if exists)

docs.title: Describe Functions
docs.text:
C-h f function-name      # Function docs
C-h v variable-name      # Variable value
C-h k C-c r              # What does this key do?

docs.title: Debug Mode
docs.text:
Add to top of init.el:

(setq debug-on-error t)

docs.title: Test in Clean Emacs
docs.text:
emacs -Q                 # Start without config

# Then manually load:
M-x load-file RET ~/.emacs.d/init.el RET

docs.title: Emacs Won't Start
docs.text:
# Move config aside
mv ~/.emacs.d ~/.emacs.d.broken

# Start fresh Emacs
emacs -Q

# If that works, reinstall:
cd ~/emacs-r-devkit
./install-init.sh

docs.title: Corrupted Packages
docs.text:
# Remove and reinstall
rm -rf ~/.emacs.d/elpa/
open -a Emacs

docs.title: Complete Reset
docs.text:
# Nuclear option
rm -rf ~/.emacs.d/
cd ~/emacs-r-devkit
./install-init.sh
open -a Emacs

docs.title: Common Error Messages
docs.text: Error Meaning Solution Package ... is unavailable MELPA unreachable M-x package-refresh-contents No such file or directory: R PATH issue C-c r P to export PATH LSP :: not in project Not in R package LSP needs DESCRIPTION file STYLER-ERROR styler missing Rscript -e 'install.packages("styler")' Buffer is read-only Special buffer Switch to different buffer

docs.title: Additional Resources
docs.text:
• Configuration Guide - Customization options
  • GitHub Issues - Report bugs
  • TUTORIAL.md - Complete user guide
  • CHEAT-SHEET.md - Quick reference

docs.title: Still Having Issues?
docs.text:
1. Run dependency checker: ./check-dependencies.sh
  2. Check Emacs version: M-x emacs-version (need 27+)
  3. Review error messages carefully
  4. Try minimal config (disable features one by one)
  5. Report issue on GitHub with error details

Last updated: 2025-12-07
